name: Solothurner Nusstorte (Solothurn Hazelnuts Ga
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg whites and sugar together until stiff peaks form, gradually adding sugar.
2. Fold in the hazelnuts and cornflour.
3. Mark two 22 cm diameter circles on a greased and floured baking tray.
4. Pipe the mixture onto the tray, covering the circles to a depth of about 1/2 cm.
5. Bake for 12-15 minutes at 160°C, then remove from the baking tray while hot.
6. Allow to cool completely.
7. Spread a thin layer of butter cream on one circle.
8. Place the sponge (recipe below) on top and spread another layer of butter cream over it.
9. Cover with the second circle and mask the sides with butter cream.
10. Coat the sides with coarsely ground hazelnuts.
11. Dust the finished gateau with icing sugar.
SPONGE:
1. Cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the egg yolks one at a time, continuing to beat.
3. Mix the flour, cornflour, and hazelnuts together in a separate bowl.
4. Alternately fold the dry ingredients into the creamed mixture with the stiffly beaten egg whites.
5. Pour into a greased 22 cm diameter baking tin.
6. Bake for 20 minutes at 160-170°C.
